title: Career Awareness Program (5th April 2025)
content: Event Date:5th April 2025 (Saturday) 08:50 AM – 09:40 AM Brief Description: The Career Awareness Program organized by the School of Computer Applications and Technology at Galgotias University aimed to guide MCA students (Batch 2024) in planning their professional journey. The session focused on company profiling, building a strong online presence, improving communication skills, learning essential technology stacks, and creating a clear career roadmap. It was designed to help students understand industry expectations and prepare effectively for future opportunities. Detailed Description: Introduction: The School of Computer Applications and Technology at Galgotias University organized a Career Awareness Program on 5th April 2025 for the first-year MCA students of the 2024 batch. This initiative aimed to help students understand the evolving job market and equip them with the necessary knowledge to make informed career choices. Objectives: The main objectives of the session were to: Provide clarity on career options available in the IT sector. Educate students on the importance of building a strong professional presence. Enhance personal and communication skills for workplace readiness. Introduce essential technology stacks that are in demand. Guide students in creating a clear and personalized career roadmap. The session began promptly at 08:50 AM with an engaging introduction on the significance of career planning. Key topics covered during the session included: Company Profiling: Understanding company types, their requirements, and how to align personal skills with organizational goals. Building Online Presence: Tips on creating a strong LinkedIn profile, contributing to GitHub, and networking with professionals. Mastering Personal & Communication Skills: Real-world advice on improving verbal and non-verbal communication. Essential Technology Stacks: Overview of programming languages, frameworks, and tools to focus on. Career Path Planning: Step-by-step guidance on setting realistic goals and mapping out a development plan. The session was interactive, with students asking questions and participating in discussions actively. Conclusion: The Career Awareness Program served as a stepping stone for students to begin thinking seriously about their future in the tech industry. It was well-received and appreciated by participants for its practical insights and career-focused approach. The department aims to continue organizing such initiatives to empower students for academic and professional excellence. Department Name:School of Computer Applications and Technology [SCAT]
